content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 uses CLLocationManager with requestWhenInUseAuthorization/startUpdatingLocation to record GPS routes, and description.txt advertises "maps every run with GPS" — a direct contradiction.

Pierre: Apple explicitly checks that the privacy policy accurately discloses data collection, and a stated denial of location collection while the app actively collects and stores GPS route data via CoreLocation is exactly the kind of contradiction reviewers flag under 5.1.1(i). This mismatch could get the app rejected or even trigger accusations of a misleading privacy policy. Fix by updating the privacy policy to disclose location collection/storage (including whether it syncs via iCloud) and ensure it matches the App Privacy nutrition label and PrivacyInfo manifest.
